title: [MEDIUM] Release architect and QA worker state after handoff
description: Deep sweep finding: completeTaskTool explicitly sets the implementation worker to IDLE before moving WORKING -> REVIEW, but submitPlanTool and qaApproveTool/qaRejectTool do not do the analogous cleanup. Because StateManager.updateTask clears assignedWorkerId on status change, the architect/QA worker can remain in READING_CONTEXT (or another active status) with currentTaskId pointing at a task they no longer own. This skews presence, team coordination, stale cleanup, and next-task claiming. Fix role handoff tools to update worker state consistently.
definitionOfDone: submit_plan sets the assigned architect worker to IDLE/currentTaskId null after plan submission/auto-approval handoff, without breaking CONTROL/SPEED/TURBO approval flows.
qa_approve and qa_reject set the assigned QA worker to IDLE/currentTaskId null when review is completed/rejected, without changing task status semantics.
Worker cleanup occurs before status changes that clear assignedWorkerId, or otherwise uses the captured prior assignee safely.
Missing worker records are handled gracefully and do not block task handoff.
Regression tests cover submit_plan TURBO and non-TURBO, qa_approve, qa_reject, and missing-worker error handling.

implementationPlan.description: In submitPlanTool, capture the pre-update assignedWorkerId and update that worker to IDLE/currentTaskId null at the appropriate handoff point. For TURBO this can happen before/after auto-approval but must use the captured assignee because updateTask clears assignment on status change. For CONTROL/SPEED, document whether the architect is idled once the plan is submitted.
implementationPlan.note: Captured the architect assignee before submit_plan status changes and idled that worker with currentTaskId=null via touchWorker before PLAN_SUBMITTED. Added TURBO, CONTROL, and missing-worker handoff regression coverage; submitPlan.test.ts passes 9/9.

implementationPlan.description: In qaApproveTool and qaRejectTool, capture the QA assignee before status update and set that worker to IDLE/currentTaskId null after successful review handoff. Handle missing worker records with a warning or silent skip, matching completeTask's non-blocking behavior.
implementationPlan.note: Captured QA handoff assignees before qa_approve/qa_reject status updates, then idled those workers with currentTaskId=null using touchWorker after the successful review status change. Missing worker records are skipped by touchWorker. Added approve/reject cleanup plus missing-worker regression coverage; qaOwnership.test.ts passes 9/9.

implementationPlan.description: Refactor a small helper if duplication emerges (e.g., idleAssignedWorker(state, task, reason)), but avoid a broad StateManager status-change side effect that could surprise plugin-driven human moves. Keep error handling local and explicit.
implementationPlan.note: Reviewed duplication and kept the cleanup local/explicit rather than adding a broad StateManager side effect or helper: submit_plan cleans up before status handoff, while QA tools use captured assignees after successful review handoff. Combined submitPlan + qaOwnership targeted tests pass 18/18.

implementationPlan.description: Add regression tests for architect worker cleanup in TURBO and non-TURBO plan submission, QA worker cleanup on approve/reject, and missing worker skip behavior. Run packages/moe-daemon npm test and npm run build.
implementationPlan.note: Added regression coverage for architect cleanup in TURBO and CONTROL submit_plan, QA cleanup on approve/reject, and missing worker skip behavior. Confirmed red first: new tests failed 4 cleanup assertions before code changes. Adversarial review adjusted submit_plan cleanup to use the captured assignee after successful PLAN_SUBMITTED to avoid idling an architect if task write fails. Verification: targeted submitPlan+qaOwnership 18/18, npm run build passed, full daemon npm test -- --reporter=dot passed 25 files / 469 tests, git diff --check clean except CRLF warnings.

planningNotes.approachesConsidered: Rejected relying on stale-worker timeout to eventually clear role workers because it leaves misleading state for up to 30 minutes and can interact badly with stale cleanup. Prefer immediate deterministic handoff cleanup.
planningNotes.codebaseInsights: StateManager.updateTask clears assignedWorkerId on status changes. completeTask already captures and idles the implementation worker before changing status; submitPlan/QA tools should follow that pattern.
planningNotes.risks: CONTROL/SPEED mode submit_plan has an awaiting-approval interval; decide whether architect remains active while waiting or is idled after submission. If idled immediately, check_approval still works by taskId/workerId. Include tests and clear comments.
